data.title: Vista Group Sets 2026 AGM, Backs Incumbent Directors but Opposes New Nominee
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Vista Group International Ltd ( (AU:VGL) ) has provided an announcement.

Vista Group International has called its 2026 annual meeting of shareholders for 21 May in Auckland, with an option for investors to attend online via a virtual platform. The agenda includes addresses from the chair and CEO, consideration of ordinary resolutions, and an open forum for general business and shareholder discussion.

Shareholders will vote on authorising the board to set auditor PricewaterhouseCoopers’ fees and on the re-election of directors Murray Holdaway and Claudia Batten, all of which have full board support. A further resolution proposes electing Stephen Mayne as a director, but the board has formally recommended that investors vote against his appointment, signalling a clear preference to maintain the current board composition and governance direction.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:VGL) stock is a Buy with a A$3.10 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vista Group International Ltd stock, see the AU:VGL Stock Forecast page.

More about Vista Group International Ltd

Vista Group International Limited operates in the cinema and entertainment technology sector, providing software solutions and related services to support film distribution, cinema management, and associated digital platforms. The company serves exhibitors and studios globally, focusing on tools that streamline operations and enhance the moviegoing and content distribution experience across international markets.

Average Trading Volume: 69,504

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$354.4M

data.title: APT Electronics’ Controlling Shareholders Plan Further H-Share Purchases
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from APT Electronics Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2551) ) is now available.

APT Electronics Co., Ltd. announced that its controlling shareholder group and senior management plan to further increase their holdings of the company’s H shares via open market purchases over the next three months. The move is based on their confidence in the group’s long-term growth prospects and investment value, while the company emphasizes that it will maintain the required public float under Hong Kong listing rules.

The controlling shareholder group currently owns about 44.04% of the company’s total issued shares, and any additional purchases will be structured so as not to trigger a mandatory takeover offer under Hong Kong regulations. APT Electronics cautions that market conditions or other unforeseen factors could limit or prevent execution of the plan and advises investors to exercise care when trading the company’s securities as it continues to provide timely disclosure on the plan’s progress.

More about APT Electronics Co., Ltd. Class H

APT Electronics Co., Ltd. is a China-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the electronics sector through its group of subsidiaries. The company issues both H shares and domestic shares and is controlled by a shareholder group that already holds a significant stake, positioning it as a key player with substantial insider ownership in its market segment.

Average Trading Volume: 78,210

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.title: Grieg Seafood Shares Trade Ex-Dividend Ahead of Split NOK 35.64 Payout
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Grieg Seafood ASA ( (GRGSF) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Grieg Seafood ASA’s shares are trading ex-dividend from 20 April 2026, reflecting a total dividend of NOK 35.63715 per share to be returned to shareholders through two separate payments. The first payment of NOK 2.04670 per share will be made as a repayment of paid-in capital on 27 April 2026, while the second and larger payment of NOK 33.59045 per share, treated as taxable and charged to free distributable equity, will be paid on 28 April 2026.

The transaction underscores Grieg Seafood’s capacity to distribute substantial cash to shareholders, signalling robust capital strength and liquidity within the company. By structuring the payout as a mix of capital repayment and taxable dividend, the company may influence investor tax profiles and potentially enhance the attractiveness of its shares to certain shareholder groups, while complying with Norwegian securities disclosure requirements.

The most recent analyst rating on (GRGSF) stock is a Hold with a NOK73.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Grieg Seafood ASA stock, see the GRGSF Stock Forecast page.

More about Grieg Seafood ASA

Grieg Seafood ASA is a Norwegian aquaculture company focused on farming and selling Atlantic salmon for the global seafood market. The company operates across several farming regions, supplying salmon to international customers and playing a significant role in Norway’s seafood export industry.

QMines Ltd. ( (AU:QML) ) has issued an update.

QMines Ltd has highlighted a transformational A$15 million strategic investment from the QIC Critical Minerals and Battery Technology Fund, designed to fast‑track development of its Mt Chalmers copper and gold project in Central Queensland. The company will outline how this capital injection supports its pathway toward production in a live investor webinar on 23 April 2026.

Alongside the funding news, QMines detailed current ore reserves and mineral resource estimates across Mt Chalmers and its additional deposits at Develin Creek, Woods Shaft and Mt Mackenzie. The enlarged resource base underpins the growth potential of the Mt Chalmers development and positions the company to leverage rising demand for critical minerals, with implications for project economics and future expansion planning.

More about QMines Ltd.

QMines Ltd is an Australian resources company focused on copper and gold exploration and development, with its flagship Mt Chalmers project in Central Queensland. The company is advancing a portfolio of deposits including Mt Chalmers, Develin Creek, Woods Shaft and Mt Mackenzie, targeting critical and battery-related metals for the energy transition.

An announcement from SECOS Group Ltd ( (AU:MCO) ) is now available.

MyEco Group Ltd has announced a proposed placement involving the issue of up to 920,000 convertible notes maturing in April 2029, with the issue date set for 20 April 2026. The transaction, disclosed in an Appendix 3B filing to the ASX, highlights the company’s use of convertible debt to access capital markets, which may strengthen its funding base and provide additional financial flexibility for future activities.

While specific use-of-proceeds details were not disclosed, the planned issuance signals continued reliance on capital-raising via listed securities, a common practice for growth-focused or restructuring companies on the ASX. data.title: Sihuan’s Xuanzhu Unit Posts Strong Phase III Lung Cancer Data for ALK Inhibitor Dirozalkib
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Sihuan Pharmaceutical Holdings Group ( (HK:0460) ).

Sihuan Pharmaceutical’s non-wholly-owned subsidiary Xuanzhu Biopharm has reported Phase III data for its next-generation ALK inhibitor dirozalkib (Xuan Fei Ning) in first-line treatment of ALK-positive advanced non-small cell lung cancer in China. The multicenter, randomized trial compared dirozalkib with crizotinib in 275 patients, assessing progression-free survival as the primary endpoint along with key response and safety measures.

The study showed dirozalkib more than doubled median progression-free survival versus crizotinib, significantly improving objective response, duration of response and disease control while delivering strong intracranial tumor responses and markedly lower brain progression risk. With a favorable safety and tolerability profile and prior regulatory approval in China for untreated ALK-positive advanced NSCLC, the robust results bolster the clinical value and competitive positioning of dirozalkib, enhancing Xuanzhu Biopharm’s oncology franchise and supporting Sihuan Pharmaceutical’s broader innovative drug strategy.

More about Sihuan Pharmaceutical Holdings Group

Sihuan Pharmaceutical Holdings Group is a Bermuda-incorporated pharmaceutical company listed in Hong Kong, operating through subsidiaries such as Xuanzhu Biopharm. Xuanzhu Biopharm, also Hong Kong-listed, is an innovative China-based drug developer focused on major diseases in digestion, oncology and non-alcoholic steatohepatitis, with dual small-molecule and biopharmaceutical R&D platforms and a pipeline spanning small molecules, biologics and ADCs.

The group concentrates on discovering, developing, producing and commercializing first-in-class and best-in-class innovative medicines backed by proprietary intellectual property. Xuanzhu’s capabilities across independent R&D, manufacturing and sales are aimed at addressing unmet clinical needs and strengthening its position as a leading innovative pharmaceutical player in China with a growing global outlook.

YTD Price Performance: 17.89%

Average Trading Volume: 35,639,862

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$13.53B

data.title: Hallenstein Glasson CEO lifts stake with on-market share purchase
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited ( (NZ:HLG) ) has shared an update.

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings chief executive James McLauchlan has increased his personal stake in the retailer, disclosing an on-market purchase of 3,500 ordinary shares on 17 April 2026. The transaction lifted his beneficial holding, held together with Lily Carlyon through JBWere (NZ) Nominees, from 5,000 to 8,500 shares at a total cost of $34,049.65.

The acquisition, made outside a closed trading period and with no use of derivatives, marginally strengthens insider alignment with shareholders at the NZX-listed clothing group. While small in scale relative to the company’s overall register, the move signals executive confidence in Hallenstein Glasson’s prospects and will be closely noted by investors tracking management’s trading activity.

More about Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited

Hallenstein Glasson Holdings Limited is a New Zealand-listed apparel retailer operating under the Hallensteins and Glassons brands. The company focuses on fashion clothing and accessories, targeting mainstream consumers across New Zealand and Australia through its store network and online channels.

Argosy Minerals ( (AU:AGY) ) has provided an update.

Argosy Minerals has called its Annual General Meeting of shareholders for 22 May 2026 at 1:30 p.m. WST in Perth, Western Australia, and will make the Notice of Meeting available digitally on its website rather than mailing physical copies. Shareholders who have not opted for email communications will receive personalised proxy forms by post and are encouraged to lodge their proxy votes by 20 May 2026, underscoring the company’s ongoing shift to electronic dissemination of corporate information and formalising governance arrangements for the upcoming meeting.
